title: How to Split a Large SQL File on Linux (Without Corrupting It)
description: Linux has powerful CLI tools, but naive splits break SQL. Here’s the safe method.
author: SQLSplit

content.text: If you split a SQL dump by bytes or lines blindly, you risk broken statements and failed imports.
SQLSplit preserves statement boundaries and output order for reliable imports.
Related: **How to Split a mysqldump File Into Smaller Parts**.

content.title: Split big dumps on Linux
content.text: Keep SQL valid, avoid import failures.

faq.question: Can I use `split -b` safely?
faq.answer: No. It can cut INSERT statements and break the import.
